skill.name: lark-whiteboard
skill.description: Create diagrams and charts in Lark whiteboard including architecture diagrams, flowcharts, mind maps, sequence diagrams, and other visual charts
skill.summary: Generate professional diagrams in Lark whiteboard using DSL or Mermaid paths with automated layout and styling
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: All 680 static analysis findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The flagged patterns exist in markdown documentation files as code examples showing CLI usage, DSL syntax, and diagram templates. The skill uses official Lark CLI tools (@larksuite/whiteboard-cli, lark-cli) for legitimate diagram rendering. No actual security vulnerabilities, malicious code, or data exfiltration risks detected.

content.user_title: Create diagrams and charts in Lark whiteboard
content.value_statement: Transform complex information into clear visual diagrams effortlessly. This skill handles layout calculations, styling, and rendering so you can focus on content.
actual_capabilities: Generate architecture diagrams with layered layouts and component relationships
Create flowcharts, mind maps, sequence diagrams, and class diagrams using Mermaid syntax
Build data visualizations including bar charts, line charts, pie charts, and treemaps
Produce analysis diagrams like fishbone diagrams, comparison matrices, and funnel charts
Render diagrams as PNG images or upload directly to Lark whiteboard documents
Apply consistent styling with color palettes, typography rules, and spacing guidelines
limitations: Complex diagrams with 50+ nodes may require manual layout adjustments
Lark whiteboard upload requires user authentication and appropriate permissions
Mermaid path provides less fine-grained control over exact positioning and styling

use_cases.title: Software Architecture Documentation
use_cases.description: Generate system architecture diagrams showing microservices, data flow, and layer relationships for technical documentation

use_cases.title: Business Process Visualization
use_cases.description: Create flowcharts, milestone timelines, and comparison matrices for stakeholder presentations and process documentation

use_cases.title: Root Cause Analysis
use_cases.description: Build fishbone diagrams and organizational charts to identify issues and present findings in team retrospectives

prompt_templates.title: Basic Architecture Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 3-layer architecture diagram showing the frontend, backend API services, and database layers for an e-commerce application. Include the main components in each layer and show the data flow between them.

prompt_templates.title: System Flowchart
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a flowchart for a user login process that includes: start, enter credentials, validate credentials decision point, success/failure branches, session creation, and end states. Use standard flowchart symbols.

prompt_templates.title: Growth Flywheel Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a growth flywheel diagram for a SaaS product showing the cycle: Acquire Users -> Activate -> Generate Value -> Retain -> Expand -> Refer -> Acquire. Use a circular layout with arrows showing the feedback loop.

prompt_templates.title: Complex Fishbone Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a fishbone diagram analyzing causes of slow application performance. Use 5 categories (Infrastructure, Code, Database, Network, External Services) with 3-4 specific causes under each. Calculate coordinates for proper bone angles and spacing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - Root cause analysis with script generation

output_examples.input: Create a simple org chart for a startup with CEO at top, then CTO and CMO reporting to CEO, each with 2 team members
output_examples.output: Organization chart diagram with hierarchical layout showing CEO at top level, CTO and CMO at second level connected with vertical lines, and 4 team members at third level grouped under their respective leaders. Clean spacing and professional styling applied.

output_examples.input: Draw a comparison matrix comparing Agile vs Waterfall methodologies across 5 criteria
output_examples.output: Side-by-side comparison diagram with two columns (Agile and Waterfall) and 5 rows (Planning, Development, Testing, Delivery, Changes). Each cell contains concise descriptions. Headers use distinct colors for visual distinction.

best_practices: Always use fit-content for height on text-containing nodes to prevent text truncation
Apply the 'outer light, inner heavy' principle: use light background colors for containers and white for content nodes
Keep connector lines in gray (#BBBFC4) and use color primarily for node fills to reduce visual noise
For flex containers, omit x/y coordinates as they are ignored - use layout property instead
Place connectors in the top-level nodes array, never nested inside frame children
anti_patterns: Setting explicit x/y coordinates inside flex layout containers - coordinates are ignored
Writing fixed numeric height values for text nodes instead of using fit-content
Nesting connector elements inside frame children instead of the root nodes array
Using colorful connector lines that create visual clutter instead of subtle gray
Overcrowding diagrams with more than 12 nodes without grouping or simplification

faq.question: What is the difference between DSL path and Mermaid path?
faq.answer: DSL path uses Lark's native JSON format for precise control over layout, colors, and positioning. Mermaid path uses standard Mermaid syntax for automatic layout of common diagram types like flowcharts, mind maps, and sequence diagrams. Choose DSL for custom layouts and Mermaid for standard diagram types.

faq.question: Do I need to install any dependencies before using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need Node.js 18+ and the @larksuite/whiteboard-cli package. Install it globally with: npm install -g @larksuite/whiteboard-cli@^0.1.0. The lark-cli tool is also required for uploading to Lark documents.

faq.question: Can I upload diagrams directly to my Lark whiteboard?
faq.answer: Yes, use the lark-cli docs +whiteboard-update command with a whiteboard token. For existing whiteboards, use --overwrite --dry-run first to check for existing content, confirm with the user, then execute without --dry-run.

faq.question: When should I use script generation vs writing JSON directly?
faq.answer: Use script generation (Node.js .js files) for diagrams requiring coordinate calculations like fishbone diagrams, bar charts, line charts, or any layout using absolute positioning. Write JSON directly for flex-based layouts like architecture diagrams and organization charts.

faq.question: How do I fix text being cut off in my diagram?
faq.answer: Set the node height to 'fit-content' instead of a fixed number. This allows the node to expand based on text content. Also check that the node width is sufficient for the text length.

faq.question: What should I do if the diagram looks crowded or overlapping?
faq.answer: Increase the gap and padding values in frame layout properties. For flex layouts, gap controls spacing between children. For absolute positioning, manually adjust coordinates or regenerate with larger spacing constants in the script.
